hooks.prompt:
Check if this tool use modifies a file in the hook/ directory (C# code). If it does, inspect the new content for these forbidden patterns:

1. Type.GetType( — Must use AvaloniaReflection instead
2. typeof(Avalonia — Must use AvaloniaReflection instead
3. System.Text.Json or JsonSerializer — Causes MissingMethodException in profiler context
4. .AddEventHandler( on RoutedEvents — Must use Expression.Lambda
5. .Content = (on ContentControl) — Causes UI freeze, must use Grid overlay
6. Enum.Parse for DispatcherPriority — It's a struct not enum in Avalonia 11+

Also check TypeScript files (src/) for:
7. localStorage. — Root runs with --incognito, data is lost

If ANY violation is found, respond with:
BLOCK: [rule number] [brief explanation of what's wrong and what to use instead]

If no violations are found, respond with:
APPROVE

Review the conversation transcript to determine if the agent made edits to source code files in hook/ (C# .cs files) or src/ (TypeScript .ts files) during this session.

If NO source code files in hook/ or src/ were edited, respond: approve

If source code WAS edited, check whether the uprooted-reviewer agent was invoked at any point during the session, OR whether /ok was run after the edits, OR whether the user explicitly said to skip review.

If source code was edited AND no review occurred AND the user didn't skip it, respond:
block — Source files in hook/ or src/ were modified but no code review was run. Invoke the uprooted-reviewer agent on the changes, or run /ok before finishing.

If a review already happened or the user skipped it, respond: approve
